summary: I was living in Pueblo, Colorado when I decided to enlist in the military. I needed a job and the military seemed like the right path for me. Pueblo was home before I became a Marine and started my journey.

I joined the United States Marine Corps because I wanted to be a Marine and needed a stable job. Boot camp was tough but I liked it, especially with instructors like Sgt. Flatter and Sgt. Brooks. Being a Marine taught me unity and watching out for each other, which was important during my deployments.

I served in the Persian Gulf War, which was a defining time in my military career. We were deployed to Kuwait and I was assigned to protect nuclear weapons aboard the USS Missouri. The war experience was intense, especially spending 112 days at sea without seeing land.

During the Persian Gulf War, I was sent to Kuwait and arrived at a port there. The scenery was beautiful but the heat was extreme. Kuwait was where we began our mission protecting nuclear weapons on the USS Missouri.

I was assigned to the USS Missouri, where I protected nuclear weapons during the Persian Gulf War. One vivid memory is going 112 days without seeing land while on board. Life on the Missouri was challenging, especially as supplies became harder to get over time.

During my service, I traveled to Spain along with other countries like Italy and Portugal. These visits were part of our deployments and gave me a chance to experience different places. We stayed unified and focused on our duties while abroad.

Italy was one of the countries I visited during my military service. Along with Spain and Portugal, it was part of our overseas stops. Despite being away from home, we maintained unity and looked out for each other.

Portugal was among the countries I went to during my time in the Marines. Traveling there with my unit was part of our routine deployments. We didn’t pull pranks or cause trouble; instead, we stayed unified and supported one another.

I also traveled to Egypt during my service, which was another stop on our deployment itinerary. Being in Egypt was part of the broader experience of serving overseas. Throughout these travels, we stayed focused on our mission and looked out for each other.

Panama was another country I visited while serving in the Marines. These trips were part of our deployments, and we always maintained a strong sense of unity. We didn’t engage in pranks but stayed professional and supportive of one another.

Australia was one of the last countries I traveled to during my military service. Like the other countries, it was part of our deployment schedule. We stayed unified and watched out for each other throughout all our travels.

After my enlistment ended, I went to work at the United States Postal Service in California. I continued working there for quite some time, and it became a steady part of my life. It was a different pace from the military, but it gave me stability at home.
cues: worked at United States Postal Service
